articles.title: Ubiquiti patches three max severity UniFi OS vulnerabilities
articles.summary: Ubiquiti has released security updates to patch three maximum severity vulnerabilities in UniFi OS that can be exploited by remote attackers without privileges. [...]

articles.title: Megalodon GitHub Attack Targets 5,561 Repos with Malicious CI/CD Workflows
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new automated campaign called Megalodon that has pushed 5,718 malicious commits to 5,561 GitHub repositories within a six-hour window. articles.summary: 1 Introduction This article provides a technical analysis of how many Windows kernel mode drivers can be interacted with from user mode without the hardware they were developed for. articles.title: ‘First VPN’ Cybercrime Service Disrupted, Administrator Arrested
articles.summary: The FBI says First VPN has been used by dozens of ransomware groups for network reconnaissance and intrusions. The post ‘First VPN’ Cybercrime Service Disrupted, Administrator Arrested appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Kimwolf DDoS Botnet Operator Arrested in Canada Over DDoS-for-Hire Attacks
articles.summary: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the arrest of a Canadian man in connection with allegedly operating a distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) botnet known as Kimwolf. articles.title: TrendAI Patches Apex One Zero-Day Exploited in the Wild
articles.summary: CVE-2026-34926 is a directory traversal flaw that can be exploited against the on-premise version of Apex One. The post TrendAI Patches Apex One Zero-Day Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Grafana Says Codebase and Other Data Stolen via TanStack Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: Hackers accessed Grafana’s GitHub repositories after a token compromised in the TanStack attack was not rotated. The post Grafana Says Codebase and Other Data Stolen via TanStack Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added two security flaws impacting Langflow and Trend Micro Apex One to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. articles.title: Cisco Patches CVSS 10.0 Secure Workload REST API Flaw Enabling Data Access
articles.summary: Cisco has rolled out updates for a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Secure Workload that could allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to access sensitive data. articles.title: Belarus-linked hackers use fake training certificates to target Ukrainian officials
articles.summary: A Belarus-linked hacking group known as GhostWriter has launched a new espionage campaign against Ukrainian government officials using fake emails disguised as messages from a popular online learning platform to deliver malware.

articles.title: Hackers steal patient and billing data from German hospitals via third-party provider
articles.summary: The large-scale data breach reportedly hit Unimed, a company that handles billing services for privately insured and self-paying patients on behalf of numerous German hospitals.

articles.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
articles.summary: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. articles.title: macOS Kernel Memory Corruption Exploit
articles.summary: A group used Anthropic’s Mythos AI model to help find a kernel memory corruption vulnerability and exploit on Apple’s M5. News article.

articles.title: UK plans for cybercrime law reform would protect almost no one, experts warn
articles.summary: The proposals would require researchers to cease activity the moment a vulnerability is identified, meaning they could not confirm it was real, assess its severity or determine its exploitability.

articles.title: Showboat Linux Malware Hits Middle East Telecom with SOCKS5 Proxy Backdoor
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Linux malware dubbed Showboat that has been put to use in a campaign targeting a telecommunications provider in the Middle East since at least mid-2022. articles.title: Chinese hackers target telcos with new Linux, Windows malware
articles.summary: A Chinese cyber-espionage campaign has been targeting telecommunications providers with newly discovered Linux and Windows malware dubbed Showboat and JFMBackdoor, respectively. [...]

articles.title: Max severity Cisco Secure Workload flaw gives Site Admin privileges
articles.summary: Cisco has released security updates to address a maximum-severity vulnerability in Secure Workload that allows attackers to gain Site Admin privileges. [...]

articles.title: Police seize “First VPN” service used in ransomware, data theft attacks
articles.summary: A virtual private network service called 'First VPN,' used in ransomware and data theft attacks, has been taken offline in a joint international law enforcement operation. [...]

articles.title: Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerability in Secure Workload
articles.summary: Insufficient validation and authentication in the Secure Workload’s REST APIs provide remote attackers with Site Admin privileges. The post Cisco Patches Critical Vulnerability in Secure Workload appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Drupal Patches Highly Critical Vulnerability Exposing Websites to Hacking
articles.summary: CVE-2026-9082 can be exploited without authentication for information disclosure, privilege escalation, and remote code execution. The post Drupal Patches Highly Critical Vulnerability Exposing Websites to Hacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Microsoft Warns of Two Actively Exploited Defender Vulnerabilities
articles.summary: Microsoft has disclosed that a privilege escalation and a denial-of-service flaw in Defender has come under active exploitation in the wild. articles.title: Microsoft Patches Exploited UnDefend and RedSun Defender Zero-Days
articles.summary: The bugs could be exploited to elevate privileges to System or create a denial-of-service (DoS) condition. The post Microsoft Patches Exploited UnDefend and RedSun Defender Zero-Days appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Google’s Surge in Chrome Vulnerability Discoveries Likely Driven by AI
articles.summary: More than 200 vulnerabilities patched in recent Chrome releases are marked as ‘reported by Google’. The post Google’s Surge in Chrome Vulnerability Discoveries Likely Driven by AI appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Supply Chain Security Crisis: Too Many Vulnerabilities, Too Little Visibility
articles.summary: New vulnerabilities are being discovered too fast, the time-to-exploitation is too short, and our visibility into them is largely lacking. The post Supply Chain Security Crisis: Too Many Vulnerabilities, Too Little Visibility appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a vulnerability in the Linux kernel that remained undetected for nine years. articles.title: GitHub links repo breach to TanStack npm supply-chain attack
articles.summary: GitHub says the hackers who breached 3,800 internal repositories gained access via a malicious version of the Nx Console VS Code extension, compromised in last week's TanStack npm supply-chain attack. [...]

articles.title: GitHub Internal Repositories Breached via Malicious Nx Console VS Code Extension

articles.title: Highly Critical Drupal Core Flaw Exposes PostgreSQL Sites to RCE Attacks
articles.summary: Drupal has released security updates for a "highly critical" security vulnerability in Drupal Core that could be exploited by attackers to achieve remote code execution, privilege escalation, or information disclosure. The vulnerability, now tracked as CVE-2026-9082, carries a CVSS score of 6.5 out

articles.title: Europe dismantles VPN service used by cybercriminals to hide ransomware attacks
articles.summary: The international operation targeted a service known as First VPN, which had been marketed for years on Russian-speaking cybercrime forums as a secure way for criminals to evade law enforcement.

articles.title: Ukraine identifies infostealer operator tied to 28,000 stolen accounts
articles.summary: The Ukrainian cyberpolice, working in conjunction with U.S. law enforcement, has identified an 18-year-old man from Odesa suspected of running an infostealer malware operation targeting users of an online store in California. [...]

articles.title: Hackers bypass SonicWall VPN MFA due to incomplete patching
articles.summary: Threat actors brute-forced VPN credentials and bypassed multi-factor authentication (MFA) on SonicWall Gen6 SSL-VPN appliances to deploy tools used in ransomware attacks. [...]

articles.title: Ukraine probes teen suspect in cyber theft scheme targeting California online shoppers
articles.summary: The investigation began after U.S. authorities informed their Ukrainian counterparts that hackers operating from Ukraine could be involved in attacks targeting users of American e-commerce platforms, Ukraine's Prosecutor General said.

articles.title: 7-Eleven confirms breach after ShinyHunters claims
articles.summary: The breach notification letters say 7-Eleven discovered the breach on April 8 and, after an investigation, determined that the cybercriminals gained access to “certain 7-Eleven systems used to store franchisee documents.”

articles.title: Grafana breach caused by missed token rotation after TanStack attack
articles.summary: The Grafana data breach was caused by a single GitHub workflow token that slipped through the rotation process following the TanStack npm supply-chain attack last week. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft Rolls Out Mitigations for ‘YellowKey’ BitLocker Bypass
articles.summary: The exploitation is mitigated by preventing the FsTx Auto Recovery Utility from starting when the WinRE image launches. The post Microsoft Rolls Out Mitigations for ‘YellowKey’ BitLocker Bypass appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: AI-Powered App Attacks Are Faster, More Frequent and Harder to Stop
articles.summary: Digital.ai’s latest threat report warns that agentic AI has erased the distinction between emerging and primary targets, enabling attackers to strike mobile apps within hours of release across every industry. articles.summary: Microsoft on Tuesday said it disrupted a malware-signing-as-a-service (MSaaS) operation that weaponized the company's Artifact Signing system to deliver malicious code and conduct ransomware and other attacks, compromising thousands of machines and networks across the world. articles.title: Identity Alone Isn't Enough: Why Device Security Has to Share the Load
articles.summary: Identity checks alone can't stop attackers using stolen session tokens and compromised devices. Specops Software outlines why Zero Trust strategies increasingly depend on continuous device verification. [...]

articles.title: Anthropic Silently Patches Claude Code Sandbox Bypass
articles.summary: The researcher who found it says the vulnerability could have been chained with a prompt injection to exfiltrate data. The post Anthropic Silently Patches Claude Code Sandbox Bypass appeared first on SecurityWeek.
